blocks.content: After school at the bike rack, the sun was still doing August things, and for about fifteen minutes it felt exactly like summer.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah. His cousin moved here. She's in seventh grade. Brooke Hadley.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She knows about the water tower.
They rode home the way they always did — Daisy taking the corner wide, Kit running the stop sign on Birch. The afternoon was easy.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Eli's already threatening a group chat.
She turned onto Magnolia. He went straight. Normal.
